mission.title: Install and Configure Charmed Kubernetes
mission.description: Charmed Kubernetes is a distribution of Kubernetes that is designed for ease of deployment and management using Juju. It is ideal for users looking for a robust Kubernetes solution that integrates well with Ubuntu and offers modern metrics and monitoring capabilities. Key trade-offs include the need for Juju for deployment and management, which may add complexity for users unfamiliar with it.

steps.title: Install Juju
steps.description:
Juju is required to deploy Charmed Kubernetes. This command installs Juju on your system.

```bash

sudo snap install juju --classic

```

> Ensure you have snap installed on your system.

steps.title: Bootstrap the Juju Controller
steps.description:
This command initializes a Juju controller which will manage your Kubernetes deployment.

```bash

juju bootstrap localhost

```

> This command boots up a local controller.

steps.title: Deploy Charmed Kubernetes
steps.description:
This command deploys the Charmed Kubernetes bundle using the specified version.

```bash

juju deploy charmed-kubernetes --channel 1.31/stable

```

> Change the channel for different Kubernetes versions.

steps.title: Verify the Deployment
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the deployed units to ensure they are active.

```bash

juju status

```

> Look for all units to be in the 'active' state.

steps.title: Post-Installation Configuration
steps.description:
Configure additional features like monitoring and autoscaling after the initial deployment.

```bash

juju add-relation kubernetes-master:metrics grafana:metrics

juju config kubernetes-worker enable-autoscaling=true

```

> This sets up monitoring with Grafana.
> Enable autoscaling for worker nodes.

uninstall.title: Remove Charmed Kubernetes
uninstall.description:
This command will remove the Charmed Kubernetes deployment and all associated data. Be aware that this will lead to data loss.

```bash

juju destroy-controller localhost --destroy-all-models

```

> This will remove the controller and all models.

uninstall.title: Clean Up Orphaned Resources
uninstall.description:
After destroying the controller, check for any orphaned resources that may incur costs.

```bash

juju remove-application kubernetes-master

```

> Remove any remaining applications manually.

upgrade.title: Backup and Upgrade Charmed Kubernetes
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, backup your current configuration and data. Then perform the upgrade and verify the deployment.

```bash

juju export-bundle > backup-bundle.yaml

juju upgrade-charm kubernetes-master --channel 1.31/stable

juju status

```

> Backup the current bundle configuration.
> Upgrade to the latest stable version.
> Verify that all units are active after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'juju bootstrap failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the Juju controller cannot connect to the cloud provider.

**Fix:**
Check your network settings and ensure the cloud provider is accessible.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'unit kubernetes-master/0 failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Kubernetes master unit failed to start properly.

**Fix:**
Run 'juju debug-log' to check for detailed error messages.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'relation not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may happen if a required relation is not established.

**Fix:**
Ensure all necessary relations are added using 'juju add-relation'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'unit is in error state'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that one or more units are not functioning correctly.

**Fix:**
Run 'juju run --all status' to get the status of all units and troubleshoot accordingly.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.31 features, including improved metrics collection and enhanced RBAC capabilities.
versionNotes.deprecations: No specific deprecations noted in this version.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 1.30, ensure that your configurations are compatible with the new RBAC settings.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'juju status' showing all units in the 'active' state, confirming that Charmed Kubernetes is running correctly.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have Juju installed and your environment meets the resource requirements.
